---
title: "02_checkm"
author: "Darius HUISMAN"
date: "2026-07-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Introduction

## Step 4. Check bin quality with checkm

cd to the top level of the workshop file (Workshop4_marine_genomics)
```bash
cd ~/marine-genomics/Workshop4_marine_genomics
```

Make a new directory for checkm and cd into it
```bash
mkdir checkm
cd checkm
```

Display the lengths of all contigs sorted from shortest to longest.
```bash
cat ../flye/bbd/assembly.fasta | bioawk -c fastx '{print length($seq),$name}' | sort -n
```
longest contig is contig_108

Once you have found the longest contig substitute its name instead of contig_XX in the code below.
```bash
mkdir bins_bbd
cp ../metabat/bins_bbd/*.fa bins_bbd/
samtools faidx ../flye/bbd/assembly.fasta contig_108 > bins_bbd/contig_XX.fa
```

create a slurm script for your checkm run
```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --time=60
#SBATCH --ntasks=6 --mem=10gb

echo "Starting checkm in $(pwd) at $(date)"

shopt -s expand_aliases
alias checkm='apptainer run -B /pvol/:/pvol /pvol/data/sif/checkm.sif checkm'

checkm lineage_wf bins_bbd out -t 4 -x fa -f checkm_results_bbd.txt

echo "Finished checkm in $(pwd) at $(date)"
```

Submit your checkm job like this
```bash
sbatch run_checkm_bbd.sh
```
(job 536)
